## Summary

Adds a public-copy scope guard so user-facing marketing/resource pages stay manuscript-first and publishing-facing.

## Why

The roadmap explicitly calls out that public copy must not reintroduce unsupported Film/TV, screenplay, adaptation, pitch-deck, producer/development-exec, or exposed internal WAVE/Gate language until those product paths exist. This PR turns that doctrine into a build-time guard.

## Changes

- Adds `scripts/guard-public-copy-scope.mjs`.
- Scans public-facing route surfaces such as:
  - homepage
  - pricing
  - resources
  - reliability / methodology / black-box / FAQ / privacy / security if present
  - Storygate / Storygate Studio
  - Agent Readiness
  - Revise
- Skips API/admin/test/internal surfaces.
- Blocks unsupported public-scope terms for screen/adaptation/product promises and internal governance breadcrumbs.
- Adds `npm run guard:public-copy-scope`.
- Wires the guard into `npm run build` before the existing spine/runtime/config/build chain.

## Scope

Guardrail only. No public copy, route UI, evaluation logic, scoring, database, Revise runtime, TrustedPath runtime, or Storygate runtime changes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Public-facing pages cannot reintroduce unsupported screen/adaptation/pitch-deck language without failing the guard.
- Public-facing pages cannot expose internal Gate/Volume/canon-gate language without failing the guard.
- Internal docs, archives, API routes, admin routes, and tests are not part of this public-copy scan.
- Build now runs the public-copy scope guard before `next build`.

## Notes

I could not run the repository locally from this connector environment. The PR is intentionally small and CI should validate the script and build wiring.
